mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate and supports equity multiples, so a sustained decline in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment reflects rising recession risk and weaker household income, which should depress near-term equity prices and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional sample mean by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth exceeds prior trend while housing starts remain depressed, the market interprets the data mix as consumption strength without broad housing recovery, supporting positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST is not positive and significant in a 60-day forward-return model, or if positive-return frequency does not exceed 55% when RSAFS rises and HOUST remains below its 6-month average.

mechanism_narrative: Improving industrial production indicates rising capacity utilization and stronger goods-sector earnings, so sustained monthly gains in INDPRO should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 1-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the rolling 2-year predictive correlation is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts rebound after a prior trough signals stabilization in interest-sensitive demand and better cyclicals breadth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 8% from its 3-month low
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts rebound signals are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of instances, or if average forward return after the trigger is not above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising CPI inflation and flat-to-higher policy rates tightens real financial conditions, compresses valuation multiples, and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases for 3 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS does not fall
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction of CPIAUCSL acceleration and non-easing FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or if triggered periods do not underperform baseline by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Weak payroll growth together with a rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and falling risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction does not forecast negative 5-day SPX returns, or if periods with falling payrolls and rising unemployment do not show below-average short-horizon returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle environment, weak GDP growth combined with weak retail sales signals an imminent demand slowdown and should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both decline versus their previous release
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in rolling out-of-sample tests.
